name: bleed-through-mid-fold
_notes: Pull the right-page corner to the spine and HOLD (no pointerup), parking the turning page near dihedral π/2 over a content-vs-content spread (j≈5 → BBB/credits). Under the polygon-offset bug fixed in PR #12, the turning page's pixels were intermittently behind the static spread along its overlap region, producing high-frequency stripes. This scenario screenshots the mid-fold and asserts the mean per-pixel luma delta in a sample patch on the turning page is below a threshold consistent with smooth shading. The threshold is conservative; tightening it requires baseline images from a known-good build.

assertions.description: Sample patch on the turning page should not contain z-fighting stripes from the spread underneath. Mean adjacent-pixel luma delta below ~28 is consistent with smooth page rendering; the bug produced visibly higher noise.
